findings.title: Critical Need for Real-Time Co-Editing
findings.description: Enterprise customers are urgently requesting simultaneous multi-user editing capabilities due to a competitor's recent launch. This feature is seen as essential for maintaining competitive positioning and preventing churn.
assumptions: Competitor launches directly influence customer decisions.

evidence.excerpt: Three enterprise accounts have submitted formal feature requests for simultaneous multi-user editing.
NPS for the collaboration workflow dropped 12 points in the October pulse survey.
Competitor X launched live co-editing... Sales has fielded 14 inbound inquiries asking when CollabDocs will support simultaneous editing.

recommendations: Prioritize development of real-time co-editing features.
Communicate roadmap and timelines to at-risk enterprise customers.

findings.title: Frustration with Document Locking Model
findings.description: The current document locking model is causing significant user frustration, leading to blocked workflows and increased reliance on external communication tools like Slack.
assumptions: Users expect seamless collaboration without manual intervention.
Current locking model is a barrier to effective teamwork.

evidence.excerpt: Support receives ~40 tickets per week from users who are blocked because a colleague has a document open.
Competitor mention rate in support tickets and NPS verbatims has risen from under 5% to 28% in three weeks.

recommendations: Implement an interim solution to reduce locking issues.
Explore user feedback on alternative locking mechanisms.

findings.title: Need for Presence Indicators
findings.description: Users are requesting visibility into who is currently viewing or editing a document to reduce duplicate work and improve collaboration efficiency.
assumptions: Users value awareness of collaborators' actions.
Presence indicators can enhance the collaborative experience.

evidence.excerpt: Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors.

recommendations: Develop and implement presence indicators as a standalone feature.
Gather user feedback on presence features post-launch.

findings.title: Conflict Resolution Strategy Needed
findings.description: As the platform moves towards real-time co-editing, a formal conflict resolution strategy is required to prevent data loss during concurrent edits.
assumptions: Data integrity is critical during collaborative editing.
Users expect robust conflict resolution mechanisms.

evidence.excerpt: A formal conflict resolution strategy is required as we move toward real-time co-editing.
CRDTs define a document as a data structure where any two states can always be merged without conflict.

recommendations: Evaluate and implement a conflict resolution strategy before launching co-editing.
Consider user education on conflict resolution features.

findings.title: Mobile Editing Experience Lags Behind
findings.description: The mobile editing experience is significantly inferior to desktop, causing frustration among users who rely on mobile collaboration.
assumptions: Mobile users expect a comparable experience to desktop.
Improving mobile functionality will enhance user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Mobile editing support for collaboration... the current document locking model on mobile causes even more frustration.

recommendations: Prioritize enhancements to the mobile editing experience.
Ensure real-time collaboration features are fully functional on mobile at launch.

summary: The analysis reveals a critical need for real-time co-editing features in CollabDocs to prevent churn among enterprise customers following a competitor's launch. Users express significant frustration with the current document locking model, highlighting the urgency for improvements in collaboration tools, including presence indicators and conflict resolution strategies. Additionally, the mobile editing experience requires immediate attention to align with user expectations.
